prompt: Act as a Senior Cloud Solutions Architect and Full-Stack PWA Engineer with extensive experience in real-time transit systems.Goal: Help me build the core architecture and MVP (Minimum Viable Product) for BiyaHero, a Progressive Web App (PWA) solving Philippine commuter and jeepney/PUV problems. The frontend will be a PWA built with Next.js (using next-pwa), the backend logic powered by C# (.NET Core), and the infrastructure hosted heavily on AWS.The app must support the following core features:Community-Sourced Routing: Users can plot and verify local commuter routes (like a wiki-map for transit).Anti-Scam Fare Calculator: Dynamically calculates exact fares based on geospatial distance (Point A to Point B) and standard base fares.Anti-123 Payment WebHooks: A payment integration mock-up that uses WebSockets to push a real-time Audio/Text-to-Speech notification to the driver's dashboard when a passenger pays via a digital wallet.Heatmap Dispatcher: A real-time geolocation ping system where commuters waiting for a ride show up as glowing hotspots on a map for nearby drivers.Success criteria:The Next.js frontend is configured strictly as a PWA (must include Service Worker setup for offline resilience and a Web App Manifest for "Add to Home Screen" capability).Clear separation of the Next.js PWA frontend and C# (.NET Core) Web API backend.AWS services are appropriately utilized (e.g., API Gateway, DynamoDB for fast reads/writes, WebSockets for real-time audio triggers).Code structure handles real-time concurrency efficiently, accounting for flaky mobile data connections using PWA caching strategies.The architecture is scalable but explained in a way that is understandable for a developer stepping into cloud engineering.Include setup instructions and a clear project structure.Use:Frontend: Next.js, Tailwind CSS, next-pwa (keep the UI mobile-first, minimal, and installable).Backend: C# (.NET Core Web API).Database: DynamoDB (for real-time heatmap) and PostgreSQL/Amazon RDS (for relational route data).Cloud: AWS architecture principles (API Gateway, WebSockets).Constraint:Focus on the PWA configuration (Service Workers/Manifest) and the real-time interaction (WebSockets) first.Avoid overcomplicating the UI; focus strictly on the "dumb/simple UI, hardcore backend" philosophy.Provide complete, runnable starter code for the Next.js PWA setup and the C# WebSocket connection for the "Anti-123" audio notification.Use comments to explain the data flow and AWS routing.Limit explanations to practical implementation details.Output:Recommended Project Structure: Clear folder hierarchy showing both Next.js PWA and C# Backend.AWS Architecture Flow: A text-based diagram/explanation of how the data moves from Next.js PWA -> API Gateway -> Backend -> Database.PWA Configuration Code: Starter code for next.config.js (with next-pwa) and manifest.json.Backend Starter Code: The C# real-time WebSocket hub for the "Anti-123" audio notification.Database Schema: How to store the community routes and live heatmap coordinates efficiently.Tone:Clear and highly technical but practical.Action-oriented and engineering-focused.Concise but complete.feel free to ask questions before proceeding for clarifications. also feel free to suggest especially on the stack if it's the right thing to do and please provide comparisons and a pro and cons. the goal is this app is we can deploy this on beanstalk or any free aws hosting service.
i did not cancel the creation. do it again.

for the MVP scopegeographic scope, yes lets focus first on metro manila but since this is community based. we must still be warry about other places.user roles, for now. yes though i think we need someone that sees all.payment integration, yes please, make it mocked for now since this just a side project.authentication, yes please, email passwordslanguages, can we make it tagalog? or there must be preferences kasi u know, not all jeepney drivers can understand English
